brief_document.title: Workflow module loading and content-hash namespacing application
brief_document.purpose: Bridge aion-package to beamr: take a validated Package, apply aion-package's content-hash namespacing to its modules, register the namespaced beams with the runtime, and record the loaded version so start_workflow can spawn the right module. This is where multi-version coexistence and replay-safe naming are realised.
brief_document.task: Implement the loader module: a load operation that consumes an aion-package Package, applies the package crate's (logical name, content hash) -> deployed name transform to every module, registers the namespaced beam bytes via the RuntimeHandle, and returns a record mapping the workflow type to its deployed entry-module name and version. Loading registers but does not run. Failures (missing entry module, namespaced-name collision from a different hash) are typed EngineError::Load with nothing partially registered. Scope is loading and naming — spawning is lifecycle (AE-009).

requirements.title: Apply namespacing and register namespaced beams
requirements.spec: WHEN loading a Package, THE SYSTEM SHALL apply aion-package's (logical module name, content hash) -> deployed module name transform to every module and SHALL register each module's beam bytes under its deployed name through the RuntimeHandle. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT re-derive the namespacing scheme itself.
acceptance: load.rs calls aion-package's namespacing transform for every module in the Package's beam set; it does not compute its own name derivation.
Each namespaced module is registered via RuntimeHandle::register_module(deployed_name, bytes).
loader/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use.
A test loads a fixture Package and asserts each module is registered under the package-crate-derived namespaced name (verified by round-tripping the transform's inverse).

requirements.title: Record the loaded version
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL record, for each loaded workflow type, the deployed entry-module name and the content-hash version, so that start_workflow for that type spawns the entry module under its namespaced name.
acceptance: load returns (or populates) a LoadedWorkflow record mapping the workflow type (logical entry module name) to its deployed entry-module name, entry function, and version identifier, derived from the Package's manifest and content hash.
The record exposes a lookup so lifecycle can resolve a workflow type to its deployed entry module/function at start time.
A test loads a Package and asserts the recorded deployed entry-module name matches the namespacing transform applied to the manifest's entry_module and the package version.

requirements.title: Multi-version coexistence
requirements.spec: WHEN two packages with different content hashes are loaded, THE SYSTEM SHALL register two coexisting namespaced module sets with no name conflict, each independently spawnable.
acceptance: Loading package A (hash h1) and package B (hash h2, same logical entry module name) registers modules under distinct namespaced names and both LoadedWorkflow records are retained.
A test loads two fixtures sharing a logical entry module name but differing in beam bytes (hence hash) and asserts both deployed names are registered and distinct, and neither overwrites the other.

requirements.title: Total load failures with no partial registration
requirements.spec: IF a Package's manifest entry module is absent from its beam set, OR a namespaced module name collides with an already-registered name derived from a different content hash, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return EngineError::Load and SHALL NOT leave any module from that package registered.
acceptance: A package whose manifest entry_module is not present among the beams returns EngineError::Load { reason } naming the missing entry module.
Registering a namespaced name that already maps to a different hash returns EngineError::Load { reason }; an identical re-load of the same hash is idempotent (no error, no duplicate effect).
On any load failure, no module from the failing package remains registered (registration is staged and only committed on full success, or rolled back on failure).
Tests cover the missing-entry-module case and the collision case and assert the no-partial-registration property.

boundaries: SHALL NOT spawn or run any workflow — loading registers modules only; spawning is AE-009.
SHALL NOT compute the content hash, validate package integrity, or define the namespacing transform — those are aion-package; this brief consumes a validated Package and the package's transform.
SHALL NOT perform hot code loading or version migration — module coexistence here is by distinct namespaced names, not VM dual-version swap.
verification: Load failures are total with no partial registration (D9).

checklist.text: load applies aion-package's (logical module name, content hash) -> deployed name transform to every module in a Package and registers the namespaced beams through the RuntimeHandle.
The loaded workflow version is recorded so start_workflow for that type spawns the entry module under its namespaced name.
Loading two packages with different content hashes registers two coexisting namespaced module sets with no name conflict.
A package whose entry module is absent, or whose namespaced module collides with an already-registered name from a different hash, fails with EngineError::Load and registers nothing.

stories.text: As a workflow deployer, I want to load a .aion package and have its modules registered under content-hash-namespaced names so that multiple versions coexist and a long-running execution keeps the exact module set it started on.
As a workflow deployer, I want a corrupt or entry-less package to be rejected on load with nothing partially registered so that a bad deploy never half-installs a workflow.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a runtime gate it at runtime, never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion depends only on aion-core, aion-store, aion-package, and beamr among workspace crates — no aion-server, no store backend crate, no networking crate. Structural; must hold.
Transport-agnostic: no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket dependency or code anywhere in the crate.
Only the runtime module imports beamr; every other module uses the RuntimeHandle (per D1).
All library errors are thiserror EngineError variants; no anyhow in this library crate. No .unwrap()/.expect() outside tests.
Mutex/RwLock poison handled explicitly and mapped to a typed EngineError; no .unwrap() on a lock guard.
WorkflowStatus is read from aion-core's event projection; the registry's cached status is never the source of truth (per aion-core CO7).
aion hardcodes no scheduler thread count, no timeout, and no retry default; configurable values come from the builder or are deferred to beamr's own default.
Module namespacing uses aion-package's transform unchanged; the engine never re-derives namespaced names (per D8).
Engine tests run against InMemoryStore; no test requires a database (per aion-core D8).

resolved_context.intention: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows, runs each as a BEAM process on an embedded beamr runtime, supervises them through a three-level tree, dispatches their activities as linked child processes, and cancels or reports on them by ID. Starting a workflow is spawning a process; cancelling it is killing that process, which through links kills its activity children too; an activity crash propagates an exit signal the trapping workflow process receives as a message. The crate is transport-agnostic — no HTTP/gRPC/WebSocket — keeping the embedded path free of network dependencies. This cluster owns lifecycle, process management, supervision, and module loading; it defers durability/replay (AD) and time/signals/queries/children/concurrency (AT) to their owners, surfacing their entry points on Engine and stating the seams explicitly.

provenance.quote: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows,
